g.text: HEADER REGION — 512 bytes (padded to sector size)
PAGE RECORD REGION — grows with each journaled page
input | page_data[4096] bytes + checksum_init seed from header
method | FNV-variant: accumulate bytes with rotate-and-XOR mixing
output | uint32_t stored at record offset 0x1004
mismatch| checksum wrong → torn write detected → stop recovery here
valid | all records before first mismatch are safe to restore
Invariant: journal fsync BEFORE any database write
Step 1 | create journal file and write 512-byte header
Step 2 | pread original page from DB file (bypass buffer pool)
Step 4 | fsync(journal_fd) ← BARRIER: original now durable on disk
Step 5 | pwrite modified page to DB file
Step 6 | fsync(db_fd) ← all modifications now durable
Step 7 | unlink journal ← THIS IS THE ATOMIC COMMIT POINT
RULE | journal MUST be durable before ANY DB page is overwritten
0x08 | int32_t | page_count (-1 while writing; patched on commit)
0x0C | uint32_t | page_size (e.g. 4096 — matches DB page size)
0x14 | uint32_t | db_page_count (total pages in DB at txn start)
0x1C | uint8_t[492] | padding[] — zeroed, fills remainder to 512 bytes
0x0000 | uint32_t | page_id — which DB page is preserved
0x0004 | uint8_t[4096] | page_data — exact original bytes from DB
0x1004 | uint32_t | checksum — integrity sentinel for record
0x0000 | uint32_t | page_id — a different modified page
